vBRIEFInfo.description: Planning and strategy chaining state for deftConsensus.

items.title: Revisit Slack integration after Phase 1 frontend isolation is complete

narrative.Context: Slack integration ideation is feasible but intentionally deferred while Phase 1 remains frontend-only with mock data and no live integrations.
narrative.FeasibilitySnapshot: Validated capabilities include channel creation (conversations.create), stakeholder invites (conversations.invite), intro messaging (chat.postMessage), PDF brief upload via upload-v2 flow (files.getUploadURLExternal + files.completeUploadExternal), and pinning key context via pinned messages.
narrative.OperationalConstraints: Channel hard-delete is generally an Enterprise Admin path (admin.conversations.delete); default lifecycle should prefer archive/unarchive for broad compatibility. Workspace policies may block create/invite/pin actions (restricted_action/no_permission). Stakeholder identity mapping must resolve internal users to Slack user IDs reliably.
narrative.SecurityAndGovernance: Use least-privilege scopes, explicit per-company/project enablement, and auditable event logs for channel lifecycle actions and membership changes.
narrative.RevisitTriggers: Start detailed planning when Phase 1 route/page work is accepted, integration policy is opened for Phase 2, and target workspace permissions/scopes are confirmed.
narrative.DecisionChecklist: Decide integration mode (notify-only vs collaboration channel), channel naming convention, lifecycle policy (create trigger and archive/delete behavior), stakeholder mapping source of truth, fallback behavior for permission errors, and moderation/retention requirements.
narrative.SuccessCriteria: A selected Slack mode can be enabled per tenant with predictable lifecycle behavior, graceful handling of permission boundaries, and clear traceability from feature status changes to Slack actions.
